input.testCaseTitle: Multi-currency refund · INR → USD conversion mismatch
input.stepLabel: Submit USD refund against INR original purchase — expect FX-quote-locked amount
input.errorMessage: AssertionError: Expected refund amount USD 12.05 (locked FX 1 USD = 83.20 INR from purchase at 2026-05-01), got USD 12.08 (current FX 1 USD = 82.95 INR). Refund used live FX quote instead of locked purchase-time FX.
input.stackTrace:
AssertionError: Expected refund amount USD 12.05, got USD 12.08
  at Object.<anonymous> (apps/e2e/specs/refund-multi-currency.spec.ts:88:21)
  at refund.service.ts:71 calculateRefundAmount()
  at fx-quote.service.ts:34 getCurrentQuote()
kbContext: Iksula Returns policy: refunds use the FX quote locked at the time of original purchase, NOT the current market FX. This protects customers from FX swings during return window.
The fx_quotes table stores quotes per orderId with TTL 365 days. Refund service must look up fx_quotes by orderId before computing refund amount in target currency.

groundTruth.rootCauseDetail: refund.service.ts:71 calls fx-quote.service.ts:34 getCurrentQuote() instead of getLockedQuoteForOrder(orderId). This violates the Iksula multi-currency refund policy that locks FX at purchase time. Fix: change refund.service.ts:71 to look up fx_quotes by orderId.
groundTruth.notesForEval: Top-2 hit on code-bug or data-bug. The bug is unambiguously code (wrong service method called) but a reviewer could call it data-bug if they interpret 'using stale-policy data source' as a data problem. Sherlock should ideally cite refund.service.ts:71 + fx-quote.service.ts:34.
